summary: Bug fix verified: Compliance Centre datetime comparison errors fixed. All 3 endpoints (/api/compliance/dashboard, /api/compliance/policies, /api/compliance/insurance) now return 200 instead of 500. Dashboard shows correct counts: 32 policies (3 active), 6 insurance docs (1 valid). UI displays all data correctly with proper status badges and action buttons.
passed_tests: Compliance Centre dashboard loads without errors
Policies tab shows 3 active policies: Safeguarding Adults, Medication, Lone Working
Public Liability Insurance shows expiry date (3/27/2027)
Public Liability Insurance has View/Download/Replace buttons
Missing insurance docs show 'missing' status with Upload button
Datetime comparison handles various formats (datetime objects, ISO strings with T, YYYY-MM-DD)
seed_data_creation: Used existing data - 32 policies (3 active: Safeguarding Adults, Medication, Lone Working), 6 insurance docs (1 valid: Public Liability)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Compliance Centre datetime bug is fixed. The fix properly handles various date formats in get_insurance_docs, get_org_policies, and get_compliance_dashboard endpoints. Key fix: datetime comparison now handles offset-naive vs offset-aware dates by checking if date is datetime object, ISO string with T, or simple YYYY-MM-DD format.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Datetime comparison errors (offset-naive vs offset-aware) in backend endpoints caused 500 errors when comparing dates stored in different formats
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Added proper date format handling in get_insurance_docs, get_org_policies, get_compliance_dashboard - checks for datetime objects, ISO strings with T, and simple YYYY-MM-DD format before comparison
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All 3 endpoints now return 200 with correct data
